meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Overcome 'Can't Finish' Syndrome (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Use this expert checklist to ship faster with Antigravity and Claude Code. Learn to build, validate, and launch like a pro.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is 'architectural paralysis.' You are over-engineering the foundation instead of shipping value. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass boilerplate, automate infrastructure, and focus exclusively on high-conversion features that solve actual user pain points, forcing your product into the market faster.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with half-finished prototypes and 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single paying user. You spend weeks configuring Auth, Stripe webhooks, and database schemas, only to lose momentum the moment you hit the actual business logic. The thrill of the idea is being suffocated by the friction of implementation.

But what if you could treat your codebase as a commodity? By adopting a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you shift your role from 'coder' to 'architect.' This is how you stop the cycle of abandonment and start the cycle of revenue. We are going to strip away the noise and get your MVP live using the latest AI-driven workflows.

steps.title: Define the Value-Driven MVP
steps.description: Don't build a platform; build a solution. Identify the single, most painful problem your users will pay to solve. We use Antigravity to map the database schema to this specific value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lean database schema for a SaaS MVP focused on [Insert Core Value Proposition]. Include only essential tables for Users, Subscriptions (Stripe-ready), and the core feature entity. Keep it normalized and scalable.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the Prisma schema based on the Antigravity output and apply migrations.'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Auth & Payments
steps.description: Never write boilerplate auth or billing logic again. Use your starter kit to inject pre-configured Auth and Stripe integration that works out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the authentication layer and Stripe checkout session for a SaaS starter kit. Ensure user roles (free vs premium) are integrated into the database schema provided earlier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe webhook handler and auth middleware as specified in the architecture.'

steps.title: Automated Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build your 'killer feature' by feeding it your existing codebase context. This ensures consistency and prevents architectural drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the functional specification for the core feature: [Feature Name]. Define the API endpoints, UI state, and data flow required for this to be production-ready.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build the frontend and API routes for the core feature based on the spec, ensuring full TypeScript type safety.'

steps.title: Rigorous Edge-Case Testing
steps.description: Most founders quit because their app breaks under load. Use AI to write unit tests for your critical billing and auth paths before launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the subscription lifecycle, specifically handling successful payments, failed charges, and cancellation events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the test suite and fix any regressions found in the authentication or billing flows.'

steps.title: Deployment & Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy to a production environment immediately. Speed to market is your only true competitive advantage. Get your MVP in front of users by EOD.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted SaaS, including environment variable management, database indexing for performance, and logging setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the Vercel deployment pipeline and verify all environment variables are correctly mapped.'

pro_tips: Never build custom UI components until you have 10 paying users; use a library like Shadcn/UI to stay focused on logic.
If you spend more than 2 hours debugging a single library, delete it and find a simpler alternative; keep your dependency graph flat.
Treat your 'Starter Kit' as an evolving product; every time you build a feature, refactor it into a reusable module for your next project.
Focus on the 'Time-to-First-Value' for your user—if they can't reach the 'Aha!' moment within 60 seconds, your feature set is too heavy.
Automate your feedback loop by integrating a simple 'Report Bug' or 'Feature Request' form that pipes directly into a Discord channel or Slack.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop coding. Spend the next 48 hours in cold outreach and content marketing to validate the business model before adding a single new line of code.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in the editor. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expertise to finally launch. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
